growth_records.medium: Fresh Water Medium (FWM), Tourna et al. 2011

up.method: colorimetric N-species tracking; near-stoichiometric NH4+->NO2- conversion

sec.method: specific NO2- production rate measured over exponential phase at 20C
specific NO2- production rate at mu_max, 20C

growth_records.cite: Ricardo J. Eloy Alves et al. Ammonia Oxidation by the Arctic Terrestrial Thaumarchaeote Candidatus Nitrosocosmicus arcticus Is Stimulated by Increasing Temperatures. Frontiers in Microbiology (2019). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2019.01571. [PMC6657660]
growth_records.snippet: Continuous cultivation... in mineral medium supplemented with antibiotics and NH4Cl or urea led to cultures with near-stoichiometric conversion of NH4+ to NO2- at 20 and 28C. Ammonia oxidation activity was most stable at 20C. Maximum specif
growth_records.notes: Values from Table 1 for Ca. N. arcticus Kfb (this study). Growth temperature optimum 28C (nitrifying range 20-28C); pH optimum 6 (range 6-7); medium pH 7.0-7.5, used pH midpoint 7.25. Temperature_C set to 20 for this record (most stable ammonia-oxidation activity). umax at 20C = 0.004-0.012 h-1 (marker-gene/qPCR based). Doubling time computed td=ln2/mu: ln2/0.012=57.8 h, ln2/0.004=173.3 h. Specific NO2- production rate 0.09-0.74 fmol/cell/h and at mu_max 0.01-0.74 fmol/cell/h (20C). NH4+ uptake near-stoichiometric with NO2- (0.5 mM NH4Cl fully converted). Inhibitory NH4+ >20 mM. Alternate substrate 1 mM urea. Per-cell rates (not per gDW) as reported.

growth_records.medium: Fresh Water Medium (FWM), Tourna et al. 2011

up.method: N-species tracking; near-stoichiometric NH4+->NO2- conversion at 28C

sec.method: specific NO2- production rate over exponential phase at 28C
specific NO2- production rate at mu_max, 28C

growth_records.cite: Ricardo J. Eloy Alves et al. Ammonia Oxidation by the Arctic Terrestrial Thaumarchaeote Candidatus Nitrosocosmicus arcticus Is Stimulated by Increasing Temperatures. Frontiers in Microbiology (2019). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2019.01571. [PMC6657660]
growth_records.snippet: Ca. N. arcticus oxidizes ammonia optimally between 20 and 28C... near-stoichiometric conversion of NH4+ to NO2- at 20 and 28C. Maximum specific growth rate (umax, h-1): 0.002-0.010 (28C); Specific NO2- prod. rate 0.19-0.86 fmol NO2- cell-1
growth_records.notes: Table 1, Ca. N. arcticus Kfb (this study), 28C condition (nitrifying optimum). umax(28C)=0.002-0.010 h-1. Doubling time td=ln2/mu: ln2/0.010=69.3 h, ln2/0.002=346.6 h. Specific NO2- production 0.19-0.86 fmol/cell/h; at mu_max 0.19-0.61 fmol/cell/h. pH optimum 6 (6-7); medium pH 7.0-7.5 midpoint used. Per-cell rates as reported (not per gDW).

growth_records.medium: Fresh Water Medium (FWM), Tourna et al. 2011

sec.method: specific NO2- production rate at mu_max, 4C; effectively no detectable nitrite production

growth_records.cite: Ricardo J. Eloy Alves et al. Ammonia Oxidation by the Arctic Terrestrial Thaumarchaeote Candidatus Nitrosocosmicus arcticus Is Stimulated by Increasing Temperatures. Frontiers in Microbiology (2019). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2019.01571. [PMC6657660]
growth_records.snippet: we repeatedly observed apparent faster growth rates (based on marker gene counts) at lower temperatures (4-8C) but without detectable nitrite production. Maximum specific growth rate (umax, h-1): 0.028-0.033 (4C).
growth_records.notes: Table 1, Ca. N. arcticus Kfb (this study), 4C non-nitrifying condition. umax(4C)=0.028-0.033 h-1 by marker-gene qPCR counts. Doubling time td=ln2/mu: ln2/0.033=21.0 h, ln2/0.028=24.8 h. Nitrite production undetectable at 4C (specific NO2- at mu_max -0.07 to 0.04 fmol/cell/h ~ zero), so growth here is NOT coupled to ammonia oxidation (authors infer alternative/heterotrophic metabolism). No ammonia uptake rate reported for this condition.
